title: CT2 automation-first Lean tactic
description: The complete compiled graph, general capability API, specialized capability profiles, node automation contracts, and semantic residual inventory for this tactic.

transitions.constructorType:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    {capability : CT2.Capability P Target} →
      {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target} →
        {input : CT2.Input capability ctx} →
          CT2.Graph.Edge capability ctx input CT2.Graph.NodeId.entry CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionDecision

{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    {capability : CT2.Capability P Target} →
      {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target} →
        {input : CT2.Input capability ctx} →
          CT2.DeletionWitness capability ctx input →
            CT2.Graph.Edge capability ctx input CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionDecision CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionC2Terminal

{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    {capability : CT2.Capability P Target} →
      {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target} →
        {input : CT2.Input capability ctx} →
          CT2.DeletionCriticalState capability ctx input →
            CT2.Graph.Edge capability ctx input CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionDecision CT2.Graph.NodeId.replacementAnalysis

{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    {capability : CT2.Capability P Target} →
      {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target} →
        {input : CT2.Input capability ctx} →
          CT2.ReplacementWitness capability ctx input →
            CT2.Graph.Edge capability ctx input CT2.Graph.NodeId.replacementAnalysis
              CT2.Graph.NodeId.replacementC2Terminal

{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    {capability : CT2.Capability P Target} →
      {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target} →
        {input : CT2.Input capability ctx} →
          CT2.SeparatingContextResidual capability ctx input →
            CT2.Graph.Edge capability ctx input CT2.Graph.NodeId.replacementAnalysis CT2.Graph.NodeId.separatingTerminal

{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    {capability : CT2.Capability P Target} →
      {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target} →
        {input : CT2.Input capability ctx} →

presentation.plainExplanation: The type of local structures in G that CT2 may delete or replace.
presentation.label: Local piece

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → CT2.PieceSystem P → P.Ambient → Type uPiece
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.Piece

presentation.plainExplanation: An exhaustive ordered enumeration of all local pieces in G.
presentation.label: Finite piece enumeration

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → (self : CT2.PieceSystem P) → (G : P.Ambient) → FinEnum (self.Piece G)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.pieces

presentation.plainExplanation: The application-defined proposition marking p as a genuine reduction candidate.
presentation.label: Proper-piece predicate

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → (self : CT2.PieceSystem P) → {G : P.Ambient} → self.Piece G → Prop
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.Proper

presentation.plainExplanation: The proposition that piece p may be reduced in the current branch state s.
presentation.label: Admissibility predicate

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → (self : CT2.PieceSystem P) → {G : P.Ambient} → P.BranchState G → self.Piece G → Prop
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.Admissible

presentation.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding whether a piece is proper.
presentation.label: Properness decision procedure

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  (self : CT2.PieceSystem P) → {G : P.Ambient} → (piece : self.Piece G) → Decidable (self.Proper piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.properDecidable

presentation.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding whether a piece is admissible in a branch.
presentation.label: Admissibility decision procedure

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  (self : CT2.PieceSystem P) →
    {G : P.Ambient} → (branch : P.BranchState G) → (piece : self.Piece G) → Decidable (self.Admissible branch piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.admissibleDecidable

presentation.plainExplanation: The type of boundary signatures through which pieces interact with their surroundings.
presentation.label: Boundary interface

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} → CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces → Type uInterface
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.InterfaceSystem.Interface

presentation.plainExplanation: Assigns to each local piece its exact boundary interface.
presentation.label: Piece interface

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} → (self : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces) → {G : P.Ambient} → pieces.Piece G → self.Interface
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.InterfaceSystem.interface

presentation.plainExplanation: The type of replaceable abstract interiors compatible with interface iota.
presentation.label: Abstract piece

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} → CT2.ContextSystem interfaces → interfaces.Interface → Type uAbstract
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.AbstractPiece

presentation.plainExplanation: The type of outside environments having boundary interface iota.
presentation.label: Outside context

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} → CT2.ContextSystem interfaces → interfaces.Interface → Type uContext
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.Context

presentation.plainExplanation: An exhaustive ordered enumeration of the outside contexts for each interface.
presentation.label: Finite context enumeration

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      (self : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces) → (interface : interfaces.Interface) → FinEnum (self.Context interface)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.contexts

presentation.plainExplanation: Reconstructs an ambient object by placing an abstract piece into an outside context.
presentation.label: Gluing operation

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      (self : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces) →
        {interface : interfaces.Interface} → self.Context interface → self.AbstractPiece interface → P.Ambient
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.glue

presentation.plainExplanation: Extracts the abstract interior represented by a concrete piece.
presentation.label: Piece abstraction

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      (self : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces) →
        {G : P.Ambient} → (piece : pieces.Piece G) → self.AbstractPiece (interfaces.interface piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.abstract

presentation.plainExplanation: Extracts the outside environment surrounding the concrete piece in G.
presentation.label: Current outside context

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      (self : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces) →
        {G : P.Ambient} → (piece : pieces.Piece G) → self.Context (interfaces.interface piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.currentContext

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that the context and abstraction recover the original ambient object exactly.
presentation.label: Reconstruction law

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.reconstruct

presentation.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding the problem's baseline property on an ambient object.
presentation.label: Baseline decision procedure

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} → CT2.Observable P Target → (G : P.Ambient) → Decidable (P.Baseline G)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Observable.baselineDecidable

presentation.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding the target property on an ambient object.
presentation.label: Target decision procedure

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} → CT2.Observable P Target → (G : P.Ambient) → Decidable (Target G)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Observable.targetDecidable

presentation.plainExplanation: Constructs the certified smaller ambient object obtained by deleting p.
presentation.label: Deletion operation

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} → CT2.ReductionOps pieces → {G : P.Ambient} → pieces.Piece G → Core.SmallerObject P G
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReductionOps.delete

presentation.plainExplanation: The type of candidate replacements available for a concrete piece p.
presentation.label: Replacement candidate

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      {contexts : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces} →
        CT2.ReplacementSystem contexts → {G : P.Ambient} → pieces.Piece G → Type uCandidate
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementSystem.Candidate

presentation.plainExplanation: An exhaustive ordered enumeration of candidate replacements for p.
presentation.label: Finite replacement enumeration

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      {contexts : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces} →
        (self : CT2.ReplacementSystem contexts) →
          {G : P.Ambient} → (piece : pieces.Piece G) → FinEnum (self.Candidate piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementSystem.candidates

presentation.plainExplanation: Interprets candidate r as an abstract piece with the same interface as p.
presentation.label: Replacement interpretation

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      {contexts : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces} →
        (self : CT2.ReplacementSystem contexts) →
          {G : P.Ambient} →
            (piece : pieces.Piece G) → self.Candidate piece → contexts.AbstractPiece (interfaces.interface piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementSystem.replacement

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that every enumerated replacement produces a strictly smaller ambient object.
presentation.label: Replacement decrease proof

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementSystem.decreases

presentation.plainExplanation: The deletion-only profile's proof that an eligible deletion preserves the baseline property.
presentation.label: Deletion preserves baseline

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {capability : CT2.Capability P Target},
  CT2.DeletionClosureRule capability →
    ∀ {G : P.Ambient} (state : P.BranchState G) (piece : capability.pieces.Piece G),
      capability.pieces.Proper piece →
        capability.pieces.Admissible state piece → P.Baseline G → P.Baseline (capability.reductions.delete piece).value
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.DeletionClosureRule.preservesBaseline

presentation.plainExplanation: The deletion-only profile's proof that a target in the reduced object lifts to the original object.
presentation.label: Deletion target monotonicity

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {capability : CT2.Capability P Target},
  CT2.DeletionClosureRule capability →
    ∀ {G : P.Ambient} (state : P.BranchState G) (piece : capability.pieces.Piece G),
      capability.pieces.Proper piece →
        capability.pieces.Admissible state piece →
          P.Baseline G → Target (capability.reductions.delete piece).value → Target G
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.DeletionClosureRule.targetMonotone

presentation.plainExplanation: The local-deletion profile's proof that an eligible deletion preserves the baseline property.
presentation.label: Local deletion preserves baseline

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {capability : CT2.LocalDeletionCapability P},
  CT2.LocalDeletionClosureRule capability →
    ∀ {G : P.Ambient} (state : P.BranchState G) (piece : capability.pieces.Piece G),
      capability.pieces.Proper piece →
        capability.pieces.Admissible state piece → P.Baseline G → P.Baseline (capability.reductions.delete piece).value
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.LocalDeletionClosureRule.preservesBaseline

presentation.plainExplanation: The local-deletion profile's proof that a target in the reduced object lifts to the original object.
presentation.label: Local deletion target monotonicity

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {capability : CT2.LocalDeletionCapability P},
  CT2.LocalDeletionClosureRule capability →
    ∀ {G : P.Ambient} (state : P.BranchState G) (piece : capability.pieces.Piece G),
      capability.pieces.Proper piece →
        capability.pieces.Admissible state piece →
          P.Baseline G → Target (capability.reductions.delete piece).value → Target G
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.LocalDeletionClosureRule.targetMonotone

presentation.plainExplanation: The explicitly supplied local reduction together with its strict rank decrease.
presentation.label: Certified smaller object

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target} → CT2.CertifiedReductionInput ctx → Core.SmallerObject P ctx.G
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.CertifiedReductionInput.reduction

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that the supplied smaller object retains the problem baseline.
presentation.label: Reduced baseline

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target}
  (self : CT2.CertifiedReductionInput ctx), P.Baseline self.reduction.value
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.CertifiedReductionInput.reducedBaseline

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that every target certificate in the supplied smaller object transports to the source object.
presentation.label: Certified target transport

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target}
  (self : CT2.CertifiedReductionInput ctx), Target self.reduction.value → Target ctx.G
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.CertifiedReductionInput.targetMonotone

apiDeclarations.type: Type
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId

apiDeclarations.type: CT2.Graph.NodeId → String
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.code

apiDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    (capability : CT2.Capability P Target) →
      (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) →
        CT2.Input capability ctx → CT2.Graph.NodeId → CT2.Graph.NodeId → Type (max uCandidate uContext)
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.Edge

apiDeclarations.type: Type
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.Terminal

apiDeclarations.type: CT2.Graph.Terminal → CT2.Graph.NodeId
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.Terminal.nodeId

apiDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    (capability : CT2.Capability P Target) →
      (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) →
        CT2.Input capability ctx → CT2.Graph.NodeId → CT2.Graph.NodeId → Type (max uCandidate uContext)
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.Path

apiDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    (capability : CT2.Capability P Target) →
      (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) → CT2.Input capability ctx → List CT2.Graph.NodeId → Prop
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.ValidTrace

apiDeclarations.type:
(P : Core.Problem) →
  (P.Ambient → Prop) →
    Type
      (max
          (max (max (max (max (max (uAbstract + 1) uAmbient) uBranch) (uCandidate + 1)) (uContext + 1))
              (uInterface + 1))
          (uPiece + 1))
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Capability

apiDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} → CT2.Capability P Target → Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target → Type uPiece
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Input

apiDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    (capability : CT2.Capability P Target) →
      (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) → CT2.Input capability ctx → Type (max uCandidate uContext)
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ExecutionResult

apiDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    (capability : CT2.Capability P Target) →
      (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) →
        CT2.Input capability ctx → CT2.Graph.Terminal → Type (max uCandidate uContext)
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.RawOutcome

apiDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    (capability : CT2.Capability P Target) →
      (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) →
        (input : CT2.Input capability ctx) → CT2.ExecutionResult capability ctx input
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.run

apiDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    (capability : CT2.Capability P Target) →
      (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) →
        (input : CT2.Input capability ctx) → CT2.ExecutionResult capability ctx input
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.runReference

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} (capability : CT2.Capability P Target)
  (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) (input : CT2.Input capability ctx),
  CT2.RawOutcome.Valid capability ctx input (CT2.run capability ctx input).outcome
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.run_verified

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} (capability : CT2.Capability P Target)
  (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) (input : CT2.Input capability ctx),
  CT2.Graph.ValidTrace capability ctx input
    (CT2.ExecutionResult.trace capability ctx input (CT2.run capability ctx input))
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.run_trace_valid

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} (capability : CT2.Capability P Target)
  (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) (input : CT2.Input capability ctx),
  ∃ result,
    CT2.RawOutcome.Valid capability ctx input result.outcome ∧
      CT2.Graph.ValidTrace capability ctx input (CT2.ExecutionResult.trace capability ctx input result)
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.run_total

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} (capability : CT2.Capability P Target)
  (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) (input : CT2.Input capability ctx)
  (left right : CT2.ExecutionResult capability ctx input),
  left = CT2.run capability ctx input → right = CT2.run capability ctx input → left = right
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.run_deterministic

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} (capability : CT2.Capability P Target)
  (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) (input : CT2.Input capability ctx)
  (result : CT2.ExecutionResult capability ctx input),
  result.terminal = CT2.Graph.Terminal.deletionC2 ∨
    result.terminal = CT2.Graph.Terminal.replacementC2 ∨
      result.terminal = CT2.Graph.Terminal.separating ∨ result.terminal = CT2.Graph.Terminal.criticality
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.outcome_exhaustive

apiDeclarations.type: Core.CapabilityContract
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.capabilityContract

apiDeclarations.type: List Core.NodeAutomationContract
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.nodeAutomationContracts

apiDeclarations.type: List Core.ResidualKindContract
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.residualKindContracts

presentation.plainExplanation: The type of local structures in G that CT2 may delete or replace.
presentation.label: Local piece

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → CT2.PieceSystem P → P.Ambient → Type uPiece
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.Piece

presentation.plainExplanation: An exhaustive ordered enumeration of all local pieces in G.
presentation.label: Finite piece enumeration

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → (self : CT2.PieceSystem P) → (G : P.Ambient) → FinEnum (self.Piece G)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.pieces

presentation.plainExplanation: The application-defined proposition marking p as a genuine reduction candidate.
presentation.label: Proper-piece predicate

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → (self : CT2.PieceSystem P) → {G : P.Ambient} → self.Piece G → Prop
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.Proper

presentation.plainExplanation: The proposition that piece p may be reduced in the current branch state s.
presentation.label: Admissibility predicate

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → (self : CT2.PieceSystem P) → {G : P.Ambient} → P.BranchState G → self.Piece G → Prop
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.Admissible

presentation.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding whether a piece is proper.
presentation.label: Properness decision procedure

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  (self : CT2.PieceSystem P) → {G : P.Ambient} → (piece : self.Piece G) → Decidable (self.Proper piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.properDecidable

presentation.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding whether a piece is admissible in a branch.
presentation.label: Admissibility decision procedure

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  (self : CT2.PieceSystem P) →
    {G : P.Ambient} → (branch : P.BranchState G) → (piece : self.Piece G) → Decidable (self.Admissible branch piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.PieceSystem.admissibleDecidable

presentation.plainExplanation: The type of boundary signatures through which pieces interact with their surroundings.
presentation.label: Boundary interface

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} → CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces → Type uInterface
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.InterfaceSystem.Interface

presentation.plainExplanation: Assigns to each local piece its exact boundary interface.
presentation.label: Piece interface

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} → (self : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces) → {G : P.Ambient} → pieces.Piece G → self.Interface
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.InterfaceSystem.interface

presentation.plainExplanation: The type of replaceable abstract interiors compatible with interface iota.
presentation.label: Abstract piece

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} → CT2.ContextSystem interfaces → interfaces.Interface → Type uAbstract
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.AbstractPiece

presentation.plainExplanation: The type of outside environments having boundary interface iota.
presentation.label: Outside context

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} → CT2.ContextSystem interfaces → interfaces.Interface → Type uContext
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.Context

presentation.plainExplanation: An exhaustive ordered enumeration of the outside contexts for each interface.
presentation.label: Finite context enumeration

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      (self : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces) → (interface : interfaces.Interface) → FinEnum (self.Context interface)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.contexts

presentation.plainExplanation: Reconstructs an ambient object by placing an abstract piece into an outside context.
presentation.label: Gluing operation

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      (self : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces) →
        {interface : interfaces.Interface} → self.Context interface → self.AbstractPiece interface → P.Ambient
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.glue

presentation.plainExplanation: Extracts the abstract interior represented by a concrete piece.
presentation.label: Piece abstraction

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      (self : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces) →
        {G : P.Ambient} → (piece : pieces.Piece G) → self.AbstractPiece (interfaces.interface piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.abstract

presentation.plainExplanation: Extracts the outside environment surrounding the concrete piece in G.
presentation.label: Current outside context

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      (self : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces) →
        {G : P.Ambient} → (piece : pieces.Piece G) → self.Context (interfaces.interface piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.currentContext

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that the context and abstraction recover the original ambient object exactly.
presentation.label: Reconstruction law

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ContextSystem.reconstruct

presentation.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding the problem's baseline property on an ambient object.
presentation.label: Baseline decision procedure

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} → CT2.Observable P Target → (G : P.Ambient) → Decidable (P.Baseline G)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Observable.baselineDecidable

presentation.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding the target property on an ambient object.
presentation.label: Target decision procedure

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} → CT2.Observable P Target → (G : P.Ambient) → Decidable (Target G)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Observable.targetDecidable

presentation.plainExplanation: Constructs the certified smaller ambient object obtained by deleting p.
presentation.label: Deletion operation

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} → CT2.ReductionOps pieces → {G : P.Ambient} → pieces.Piece G → Core.SmallerObject P G
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReductionOps.delete

presentation.plainExplanation: The type of candidate replacements available for a concrete piece p.
presentation.label: Replacement candidate

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      {contexts : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces} →
        CT2.ReplacementSystem contexts → {G : P.Ambient} → pieces.Piece G → Type uCandidate
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementSystem.Candidate

presentation.plainExplanation: An exhaustive ordered enumeration of candidate replacements for p.
presentation.label: Finite replacement enumeration

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      {contexts : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces} →
        (self : CT2.ReplacementSystem contexts) →
          {G : P.Ambient} → (piece : pieces.Piece G) → FinEnum (self.Candidate piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementSystem.candidates

presentation.plainExplanation: Interprets candidate r as an abstract piece with the same interface as p.
presentation.label: Replacement interpretation

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {pieces : CT2.PieceSystem P} →
    {interfaces : CT2.InterfaceSystem pieces} →
      {contexts : CT2.ContextSystem interfaces} →
        (self : CT2.ReplacementSystem contexts) →
          {G : P.Ambient} →
            (piece : pieces.Piece G) → self.Candidate piece → contexts.AbstractPiece (interfaces.interface piece)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementSystem.replacement

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that every enumerated replacement produces a strictly smaller ambient object.
presentation.label: Replacement decrease proof

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementSystem.decreases

presentation.plainExplanation: The deletion-only profile's proof that an eligible deletion preserves the baseline property.
presentation.label: Deletion preserves baseline

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {capability : CT2.Capability P Target},
  CT2.DeletionClosureRule capability →
    ∀ {G : P.Ambient} (state : P.BranchState G) (piece : capability.pieces.Piece G),
      capability.pieces.Proper piece →
        capability.pieces.Admissible state piece → P.Baseline G → P.Baseline (capability.reductions.delete piece).value
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.DeletionClosureRule.preservesBaseline

presentation.plainExplanation: The deletion-only profile's proof that a target in the reduced object lifts to the original object.
presentation.label: Deletion target monotonicity

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {capability : CT2.Capability P Target},
  CT2.DeletionClosureRule capability →
    ∀ {G : P.Ambient} (state : P.BranchState G) (piece : capability.pieces.Piece G),
      capability.pieces.Proper piece →
        capability.pieces.Admissible state piece →
          P.Baseline G → Target (capability.reductions.delete piece).value → Target G
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.DeletionClosureRule.targetMonotone

presentation.plainExplanation: The local-deletion profile's proof that an eligible deletion preserves the baseline property.
presentation.label: Local deletion preserves baseline

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {capability : CT2.LocalDeletionCapability P},
  CT2.LocalDeletionClosureRule capability →
    ∀ {G : P.Ambient} (state : P.BranchState G) (piece : capability.pieces.Piece G),
      capability.pieces.Proper piece →
        capability.pieces.Admissible state piece → P.Baseline G → P.Baseline (capability.reductions.delete piece).value
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.LocalDeletionClosureRule.preservesBaseline

presentation.plainExplanation: The local-deletion profile's proof that a target in the reduced object lifts to the original object.
presentation.label: Local deletion target monotonicity

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {capability : CT2.LocalDeletionCapability P},
  CT2.LocalDeletionClosureRule capability →
    ∀ {G : P.Ambient} (state : P.BranchState G) (piece : capability.pieces.Piece G),
      capability.pieces.Proper piece →
        capability.pieces.Admissible state piece →
          P.Baseline G → Target (capability.reductions.delete piece).value → Target G
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.LocalDeletionClosureRule.targetMonotone

presentation.plainExplanation: The explicitly supplied local reduction together with its strict rank decrease.
presentation.label: Certified smaller object

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target} → CT2.CertifiedReductionInput ctx → Core.SmallerObject P ctx.G
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.CertifiedReductionInput.reduction

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that the supplied smaller object retains the problem baseline.
presentation.label: Reduced baseline

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target}
  (self : CT2.CertifiedReductionInput ctx), P.Baseline self.reduction.value
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.CertifiedReductionInput.reducedBaseline

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that every target certificate in the supplied smaller object transports to the source object.
presentation.label: Certified target transport

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target}
  (self : CT2.CertifiedReductionInput ctx), Target self.reduction.value → Target ctx.G
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.CertifiedReductionInput.targetMonotone
